content: We can borrow the lexical teaching framework of 'reflexion' as both a noun (static reflection result) and a transitive/intransitive verb (active reflection action) to restructure current LLM self-reflection mechanisms, splitting them into two decoupled modules: static reflexion memory storage that records past reflection outcomes, and active reflexion processing that executes self-questioning and error correction. This split solves the common problem that current LLM self-reflection systems conflate memory storage and reflective processing, leading to repetitive or stale self-correction.
